name: Pont Simone-Veil Approach
roadSegment: Pont Simone Veil / Bordeaux entry, about 4.5 km from finish
summary: Bordeaux approach option on the new wide bridge, trading finish-line ceremony for easier viewing before the sprint finish compression.
whyItMatters: The official schedule names Pont Simone Veil 4.5 km from the finish; a pre-finish point may be more practical than the finish barrier if public access is confirmed.

access.estimated_route_from_base: Approach from Bordeaux/Begles/Floirac side on foot or by transit before closures; exact hotel/base not assumed.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Urban transit/walking preferred. Parking near the bridge or final route is unknown during closures.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: The bridge normally includes pedestrian/cycling public space, but race-day spectator zones and crossing rules are unknown.

pros: Official schedule point before Bordeaux finish, with clear timing.
Wide bridge/public-space design may offer more breathing room than narrow final-kilometer barriers if public areas remain open.
Good escape potential toward either bank compared with the finish line, pending closures.
cons: Bridge access could be closed or restricted for race security.
Riders may be very fast on a flat finish approach.
Standing on a bridge can create pinch points and may have police restrictions.

logistics.parking: Unknown; do not plan on nearby bridge parking.
logistics.transit: TBM urban network nearby in normal conditions, but race-day reroutes are unknown.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs Bordeaux/Gironde closure map and bridge pedestrian-access rules.

crowdRisk: medium-high: official Bordeaux entry landmark close to the finish, but likely less intense than finish-line barriers.
bathroomFoodProximity: Unknown on race day; nearby urban facilities may be accessible on either bank but must be checked against barriers.
escapeRoute: After the convoy, walk away from the bridge toward the same bank's transit/open streets; avoid depending on an immediate cross-course crossing.
safetyAssessment: Use only authorized pedestrian/spectator zones. Do not stand on the roadway, median, bridge edge pinch points, or cycle lane if it is part of event control.

timing.best_arrival_window: 4 hours before expected passage if targeting front-row bridge/approach viewing.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium-high until bridge access and transit changes are published.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 7 official page
sources.note: Supports Stage 7 route, Pont Simone Veil schedule point, and expected Bordeaux arrival timing.

sources.title: Pont Simone-Veil background
sources.note: Supports bridge coordinates, Bordeaux/Begles/Floirac location, width, pedestrian/cycling public-space context, and opening background; not a race-day closure source.

sources.title: Bordeaux tramway background
sources.note: Supports general TBM/tram urban-transit context in Bordeaux; specific race-day service changes still need official verification.

open_questions: Will spectators be allowed on Pont Simone-Veil or only on approach sidewalks?
Which TBM lines/stops are operating or rerouted near the bridge on Stage 7?
Where are official toilets, water points, or fan zones near the bridge?
